_meta.note: Mixed cooking-appliance DB: ranges (slide-in/freestanding), wall ovens, cooktops. Fuel: induction is the major 2025-26 trend (DOE rebates up to $840). gas | electric | induction | dual_fuel. Yale 2026 reliability: induction service rates higher than gas due to electronics complexity. Output BTU for gas burners; wattage for induction.
_meta.honesty_pass: Unverified rating fields nulled on 2026-04-24. Numeric scores (cr_overall, reviewed, rtings, yale_reliability_pct, etc.) will be repopulated as they are confirmed against primary sources; source URLs will be added to ratings.source_urls.

models.name: LG LSEL6337F Induction Slide-in
pros: Yale 2026 most-reliable induction range (4.6% service rate)
cons: Glass scratches if pots are dragged — lift, don't slide

models.name: Thermador Pro Harmony Liberty 30" Induction Range
pros: Liberty freeform cooktop — pot placement anywhere across 56 inductors
Three flex zones detect pot size, shape, and position
Pro Harmony aesthetic with adjustable color touchscreen
cons: $9k MSRP — premium pricing without independent reliability data in our DB
4.4 cf oven smaller than mainstream 30" induction (LG 6.3 cf, Samsung 6.3 cf)

models.name: JennAir RISE 30" Induction Professional-Style Range
pros: Flex-Choice induction zones (two separate elements or one large surface)
Culinary Center remote access via WiFi
cons: 4.1 cf oven typical of pro-style (smaller than slide-in equivalents)
Two-year limited warranty short for the tier

models.name: JennAir RISE 36" Induction Professional-Style Range
pros: Flex-Choice induction zones with Assisted Cooking presets
Halo-Effect knobs + edge-to-edge black glass cooktop
cons: $15k MSRP enters Wolf/Miele territory without their reliability track record

models.name: Wolf 30" Transitional Induction Range
pros: Bridge function joins zones for griddles, fish poachers, oblong cookware
10 cooking modes including Gourmet (~50 presets), Stone, Proof, Dehydrate
Dual VertiFlow convection for even multi-rack baking
cons: Transitional styling has less pro-look than IR36551
$9.9k MSRP — Wolf premium without independent Yale reliability data

models.name: Wolf 36" Professional Induction Range
pros: New 5500W boost zone for fastest boil times in class
Pro red knobs + iconic Wolf design
cons: Pro-styling requires kitchen built around it
